articles.title: Grounding Techniques for Anxiety Before a Retreat
articles.summary: Simple somatic, breath, and mindset practices you can use in the weeks leading up to a retreat to keep anxiety from taking over.
articles.body:
Preparing for a retreat often stirs up excitement and nervousness at the same time. For many people living with anxiety, uncertainty about travel, new people, and unfamiliar routines can amplify worry.

In holistic therapy, we approach pre-retreat anxiety with compassionate curiosity rather than resistance. The aim is not to get rid of anxious feelings but to give your nervous system more options.

**1. Orienting to Safety**
Spend 1–2 minutes a few times a day slowly looking around the space you are in. Name 5 things you see, 4 things you can touch, 3 things you can hear, 2 things you can smell, and 1 thing you can taste. This simple orientation exercise tells your brain that you are in the present, not in an imagined scenario.

**2. 4–6 Grounding Breath**
Inhale through the nose for a count of 4, pause briefly, and exhale through the mouth for a count of 6. The slightly longer exhale engages the parasympathetic nervous system, supporting a shift from fight‑or‑flight towards rest‑and‑digest.

**3. Supportive Self-Talk**
Write down the most anxious thoughts you have about attending the retreat. For each one, add a compassionate response you might offer a close friend. Keep this list on your phone or in a journal to revisit when anxiety spikes.

**4. Micro-Rehearsals**
If social anxiety is part of your experience, practice short, low‑stakes interactions in daily life: greeting a barista, asking a colleague about their day, or making brief eye contact in a safe context. These micro‑rehearsals build confidence in your capacity to connect.

Working with a trauma‑informed therapist or coach before and after the retreat can deepen these practices. Remember: you don’t have to feel perfectly calm to benefit from a retreat; you only need enough support to stay connected to yourself as you step into something new.

articles.title: How Holistic Therapy Supports Generalized Anxiety Day to Day
articles.summary: Explore how body-based practices, nervous-system education, and relational support come together to ease generalized anxiety.
articles.body:
Generalized Anxiety Disorder (GAD) is often described as chronic, excessive worry that feels difficult to control. While medication and traditional talk therapy can be very helpful, many people also benefit from holistic approaches that include the body, emotions, and environment.

**Working With the Nervous System**
Holistic therapy focuses on building a relationship with your nervous system. We explore how stress shows up in your muscles, breath, and posture, and we introduce practices like gentle movement, mindful breathing, and grounding touch.

**Integrating Lifestyle and Ritual**
Small daily rituals can act as "anchors" in the midst of anxiety: lighting a candle before starting work, taking a mindful walk after lunch, or stretching before bed. These rituals encourage your system to expect moments of safety and ease.

**Community and Belonging**
Group circles, online programs, and retreats offer relational healing—learning that you are not alone in your experience. Being witnessed in a non‑judgmental space can reduce the shame that often accompanies anxiety.

Holistic therapy is not a quick fix, but over time it can gently reshape how you relate to anxious thoughts, making space for more freedom and choice.

articles.title: A 15-Minute Morning Mindfulness Routine for Anxiety Relief
articles.summary: Create a gentle morning routine that soothes anxiety before the day begins, using mindfulness, journaling, and light movement.
articles.body:
How you start your morning can influence your anxiety levels for the entire day. A short, intentional routine can send a powerful signal of safety to your nervous system.

**Minutes 1–3: Arrival**
Sit comfortably, close your eyes if that feels safe, and notice three sensations in your body without trying to change them. Name them silently: "warm hands," "tight chest," "buzzing legs."

**Minutes 4–7: Anchored Breathing**
Place one hand on your belly and inhale gently through the nose, feeling the hand rise. Exhale slowly through the mouth. Count 8 breaths, letting your attention rest on the feeling of your hand moving.

**Minutes 8–11: Gentle Movement**
Stand and explore slow neck rolls, shoulder circles, and side stretches. Move only as far as is comfortable. The goal is to invite mobility, not to force a workout.

**Minutes 12–15: Intentional Journaling**
On paper, complete these three prompts:
1. "Today, I’m willing to be kind to myself by…"
2. "I expect my anxiety might show up when…"
3. "If it does, I will support myself by…"

Repeating this routine for 3–4 weeks creates a predictable rhythm that many people with anxiety find deeply regulating, especially when combined with therapy or group support.

articles.title: Restful Sleep After Intense Healing Work
articles.summary: Why deep emotional processing can temporarily disrupt sleep—and how to support your body in integrating safely.
articles.body:
After a powerful therapy session or retreat day, many people notice changes in their sleep: vivid dreams, restlessness, or waking in the night. This doesn’t mean something is wrong—it often means your system is integrating.

**Why Sleep Shifts After Healing Work**
Emotional processing involves the limbic system, which is also active during dreaming. As your brain reorganizes memories and sensations, sleep can feel lighter or more fragmented for a short period.

**Sleep-Supportive Practices**
- **Warm-down, not just wind-down:** Give yourself at least 30–45 minutes between screens and bedtime to let your body "cool" from emotional intensity.
- **Containment journaling:** Before sleep, write down what feels unfinished and end with the sentence: "For tonight, it is enough to rest." This signals to your nervous system that processing can pause.
- **Nervous-system-friendly comfort:** Weighted blankets, gentle stretching, or a brief body scan can help re‑anchor you in the present.

If disrupted sleep continues for more than a few weeks or is accompanied by high distress, reach out to a therapist or healthcare provider. Otherwise, with supportive practices, most people find that their sleep deepens again—and often becomes more restorative than before.

articles.title: Sleep Hygiene Tips to Get the Most From Your Retreat
articles.summary: Practical sleep hygiene strategies you can use before and during a retreat so your body can fully receive the benefits.
articles.body:
Good sleep is one of the most underrated components of a transformative retreat. While late‑night conversations can be beautiful, chronic sleep debt makes emotional processing harder and can increase anxiety.

**Before the Retreat**
Try to shift your bedtime 15 minutes earlier every few nights in the week leading up to your trip. Arriving with a rested nervous system will help you adjust more easily to the new environment.

**During the Retreat**
- Create a simple bedtime ritual you can replicate in your room: a short stretch, a cup of herbal tea, or a two‑minute gratitude practice.
- If noise is a concern, pack earplugs or a small white‑noise machine app.

**If Sleep is Difficult**
Instead of forcing sleep, focus on rest. Place a hand on your chest and one on your belly, breathe slowly, and remind yourself: "Even resting is supportive right now." This reduces pressure and often allows sleep to come more naturally.

If you live with insomnia or sleep‑related anxiety, consider speaking with your retreat facilitators ahead of time so they can help you plan extra support.

articles.title: What Makes a Yoga Class Trauma-Informed?
articles.summary: Key principles that distinguish trauma-informed yoga from conventional classes and why they matter for healing.
articles.body:
Trauma‑informed yoga is less about specific poses and more about how the space is held. Its core intention is to offer choice, safety, and empowerment for people whose nervous systems have learned to be on guard.

**Core Principles**
- **Choice over compliance:** Students are invited to notice how movements feel and are welcomed to opt out or modify at any time.
- **Neutral language:** Cues avoid commanding or triggering language and instead focus on options and curiosity.
- **Attention to the environment:** Lighting, music, and room setup are chosen with care so participants can orient easily and feel a sense of safety.

In our trainings and retreats, we weave trauma‑informed principles into yoga, meditation, and group sharing, recognizing that every body carries its own history and wisdom.

articles.title: Breathwork and the Nervous System: A Gentle Introduction
articles.summary: Understand how different breath patterns influence your nervous system and how to practice safely.
articles.body:
Breathwork has become increasingly popular—from short online webinars to immersive in‑person sessions. At its heart, breathwork is about using conscious breathing to influence your state of being.

**Regulating vs. Activating Practices**
Not all breathwork is the same. Some practices are calming, while others are intentionally activating.
- **Regulating examples:** extended exhale breathing, box breathing, gentle diaphragmatic breathing.
- **Activating examples:** holotropic‑style breathing, fast rhythmic breathing, and certain rebirthing techniques.

**Safety Considerations**
If you live with anxiety, trauma, or a history of panic attacks, it’s especially important to start with gentle practices and work with trained facilitators. Online webinars can be a safe way to learn foundations before exploring deeper sessions at a retreat.

Always listen to your body; you can slow down, pause, or stop at any time.

articles.title: Planning Your First Holistic Retreat: A Practical Checklist
articles.summary: From budgeting and travel to emotional preparation, here’s how to choose and prepare for your first holistic retreat.
articles.body:
Choosing your first retreat can feel overwhelming: there are so many locations, themes, and price points. A simple checklist can make the process less stressful.

**Clarify Your Intention**
Ask yourself: What kind of support am I seeking—rest, trauma healing, spiritual exploration, or skill‑building? Your intention will guide whether you choose a meditation weekend, a trauma‑informed training retreat, or a yoga and nature immersion.

**Budget and Duration**
Look honestly at your financial and time resources. Shorter weekend retreats can be powerful and more accessible. Pay attention to what’s included in the price: meals, accommodation, and transportation.

**Emotional Preparation**
If you live with anxiety or complex trauma, consider scheduling a session with your therapist before and after the retreat. Identify grounding tools you can lean on during challenging moments.

Print or save this checklist and revisit it as you explore different retreat options on our site.

articles.title: How to Choose a Trauma-Sensitive Retreat in Europe
articles.summary: Key questions to ask when selecting a trauma-sensitive retreat in Europe, from location and group size to facilitator training.
articles.body:
Europe offers a wide range of holistic retreats—from seaside mindfulness weekends in Portugal to nature‑immersed programs in the UK countryside. If you’re seeking trauma‑sensitive support, a few extra questions can help ensure a good fit.

**Questions to Ask**
- How are facilitators trained in trauma‑informed care?
- What is the maximum group size?
- Are there quiet spaces or one‑on‑one support options during the retreat?

Consider your preferences around climate, culture, and travel time. For some, flying across continents is worth it; for others, a shorter trip reduces stress and cost.

Use our "Retreats in Europe" filter and read the "What’s included" section carefully to see how each retreat supports emotional safety as well as spiritual growth.

articles.title: What to Expect from a Holistic Massage at Our Los Angeles Center
articles.summary: A step‑by‑step look at our holistic massage sessions in Los Angeles, including intake, session flow, and aftercare tips.
articles.body:
Holistic massage at our Los Angeles Center is designed to support your whole system—body, mind, and emotions.

**Before Your Session**
You’ll complete a brief intake form and have a short conversation with your therapist about your goals, areas of tension, and any injuries or trauma‑related considerations.

**During the Session**
Your therapist will check in about pressure and comfort. You are encouraged to communicate at any time and can request silence, music, or guided breath cues.

**Aftercare**
We recommend drinking water, moving slowly for the rest of the day, and making notes about emotions or insights that arise. Many guests choose to pair massage sessions with meditation or gentle yoga classes at the center.

articles.title: Is an Online Holistic Therapy Training Right for You?
articles.summary: Compare in‑person and online holistic therapy trainings, including cost, schedule, and learning style considerations.
articles.body:
Online holistic therapy trainings have opened new pathways for students who can’t travel for extended periods. But are they right for you?

**Benefits of Online Training**
- Lower overall cost (no flights or accommodation)
- Flexible scheduling, especially for evening cohorts
- The ability to revisit recorded sessions

**When In-Person May Be Better**
If you crave immersive, embodied practice and in‑person feedback, an on‑site intensive or retreat‑based program may be more aligned. Many students choose a hybrid path: starting online, then deepening with an in‑person retreat module.

Reflect on your schedule, learning style, and financial needs as you explore our upcoming training listings.

articles.title: Designing a Nourishing Morning Retreat at Home
articles.summary: Use yoga, mindfulness, and intention-setting to create a 3‑workshop morning retreat you can do from home.
articles.body:
You don’t always have to travel to feel the benefits of a retreat. A thoughtfully designed morning can offer surprising depth.

**Choose Three Pillars**
We recommend combining:
1. **Yoga or movement** to awaken the body.
2. **Mindfulness or meditation** to center your mind.
3. **An integration practice** such as journaling, creative expression, or guided reflection.

Schedule each for 45–60 minutes with short breaks in between. Keep your phone on airplane mode, prepare a simple nourishing breakfast, and let loved ones know you’ll be "on retreat" for the morning.

articles.title: Trauma-Informed Therapy: Beyond Buzzwords
articles.summary: A clear explanation of what trauma‑informed therapy is—and is not—plus how to know if a practitioner is truly trauma‑aware.
articles.body:
The phrase "trauma‑informed" is widely used, but not always well understood. At its core, trauma‑informed therapy recognizes how overwhelming experiences shape the nervous system, relationships, and sense of self.

**Key Elements**
- **Safety:** emotional, physical, cultural, and relational safety are prioritized.
- **Choice:** clients are offered options and can slow down or pause at any time.
- **Collaboration:** healing is something you co‑create, not something done to you.

Ask potential therapists about their specific trauma training, supervision, and how they adapt sessions for people with anxiety, dissociation, or complex trauma histories.

articles.title: Untangling Burnout, Anxiety, and Sleep Issues
articles.summary: Learn how burnout, chronic anxiety, and disrupted sleep feed into each other—and which small changes help most.
articles.body:
Burnout rarely appears alone. It often travels with heightened anxiety and sleep challenges, creating a cycle that’s hard to break.

**The Burnout–Anxiety–Sleep Loop**
When you’re overwhelmed, your nervous system stays on high alert. This makes it harder to wind down at night, which leads to poorer sleep. Poor sleep then increases anxiety and reduces your capacity to cope with daily demands.

**Breaking the Cycle Gently**
Instead of overhauling your entire routine, start with one or two small changes:
- Choose a consistent bedtime window.
- Add a five‑minute breath or body scan practice.
- Schedule one weekly activity that nourishes you (like a yoga class or nature walk).

If possible, consider a short retreat or online program specifically focused on nervous‑system regulation. Stepping away from your usual environment can create space for new patterns to emerge.

training_programs.title: 6-Week Online Holistic Therapy Foundations
training_programs.subtitle: Nervous-system aware skills for supporting clients with anxiety and stress
training_programs.short_description: A live, evening online program covering core holistic therapy principles, somatic basics, and trauma-informed communication.
training_programs.long_description:
This 6-week live online training introduces the foundations of holistic therapy with a strong focus on anxiety, burnout, and sleep-related challenges. Each week blends nervous-system education, simple somatic practices, and reflective exercises you can use with clients or in your own life.

Topics include: holistic assessment, trauma-informed principles, working with anxiety and sleep disruption, ethical scope of practice, and integrating movement and breath safely. Sessions are held in small interactive groups with time for Q&A and practice.

training_programs.title: 6-Week Online Trauma-Informed Yoga Essentials
training_programs.subtitle: Apply trauma-informed principles to group and 1:1 yoga settings
training_programs.short_description: Learn to design yoga classes rooted in choice, safety, and nervous-system awareness in this interactive online training.
training_programs.long_description:
Guided by experienced trauma-informed yoga facilitators, this 6-week online program explores how to bring trauma-informed principles into your teaching. You will learn about the neurobiology of trauma, choice-based cueing, adapting postures, and supporting dysregulated students.

Weekly live calls include lecture, practice teaching, and reflective inquiry. Optional mentoring calls and peer practice groups help you integrate what you learn into your own context.

training_programs.title: 6-Week Online Breathwork for Emotional Regulation
training_programs.subtitle: Foundational breathwork training for coaches and therapists
training_programs.short_description: A practice-focused breathwork training emphasizing gentle, regulation-oriented techniques suitable for anxious and trauma-affected clients.
training_programs.long_description:
This 6-week online program trains you to facilitate gentle breathwork safely. You will learn the difference between activating and regulating practices, key contraindications, and how to adapt for anxiety, panic, and trauma histories.

Weekly modules include live demonstrations, supervised practice in breakout rooms, and case discussions. You�b4ll leave with a toolkit of short, accessible breath practices to integrate into sessions or classes.

training_programs.title: 8-Week Online Clinical Tools for Anxiety & Sleep
training_programs.subtitle: Evidence-informed, holistic approaches to chronic anxiety and insomnia
training_programs.short_description: For therapists and counselors seeking integrative tools for anxiety and sleep challenges, blending CBT-I concepts with somatic and mindfulness practices.
training_programs.long_description:
Across eight weeks you�b4ll deepen your understanding of anxiety and sleep from both a clinical and holistic perspective. The curriculum includes psychoeducation, behavioral sleep strategies, somatic tools, and client-friendly metaphors for explaining nervous-system dynamics.

Live calls combine didactic teaching with demonstrations and small-group role play, with optional consultation hours for complex cases.

training_programs.title: 12-Week Hybrid Trauma-Informed Practitioner Pathway
training_programs.subtitle: Blending online learning with a 5-day in-person retreat intensive
training_programs.short_description: A comprehensive training path for practitioners integrating trauma-informed principles into therapy, coaching, or yoga teaching.
training_programs.long_description:
This 12-week hybrid program combines weekly online study with a 5-day in-person immersion at one of our retreat centers. Modules explore the foundations of trauma theory, attachment, dissociation, and somatic stabilization, as well as practical tools for group work and 1:1 sessions.

Participants complete live webinars, peer-practice assignments, and reflective journaling online before gathering in person for intensive practice, supervision, and personal process work.

training_programs.title: 4-Week In-Person Meditation Facilitator Intensive
training_programs.subtitle: Trauma-sensitive meditation and circle holding skills
training_programs.short_description: A month-long, in-person intensive for aspiring meditation facilitators who want to lead circles with care and skill.
training_programs.long_description:
Hosted at selected centers, this 4-week intensive weaves daily meditation practice with facilitation labs, group dynamics education, and trauma-sensitive adaptations. Participants gain experience leading short practices, giving and receiving feedback, and holding closing circles.

The program includes mentoring, peer supervision, and a closing integration plan for returning to your community.

training_programs.title: 10-Week Online Mindful Movement for Therapists
training_programs.subtitle: Bring simple movement into talk therapy and coaching sessions
training_programs.short_description: An experiential training for clinicians who want to integrate chair-based and gentle standing movement into their work with clients.
training_programs.long_description:
Over ten weeks, you�b4ll learn accessible, trauma-aware movement sequences suitable for offices and telehealth. Topics include working with consent and choice, pacing movement with client arousal levels, and adapting for mobility differences.

No prior yoga or movement background is required, only a willingness to explore and practice in community.

training_programs.title: 6-Week Online Retreat Design & Facilitation Lab
training_programs.subtitle: Create trauma-sensitive retreats and custom morning immersions
training_programs.short_description: Learn to design, price, and facilitate small retreatsincluding custom morning itineraries combining yoga, mindfulness, and integration sessions.
training_programs.long_description:
Ideal for yoga teachers, coaches, and emerging facilitators, this 6-week lab walks you through designing retreats that are both financially sustainable and psychologically safe. You�b4ll learn how to structure schedules, build in integration time, and offer tiered pricing and room options.

Participants prototype a retreat or mini-immersion of their own and receive supportive feedback from peers and faculty.

training_programs.title: 3-Week Online Intro to Gentle Breathwork
training_programs.subtitle: Foundations for personal practice and entry-level facilitation
training_programs.short_description: A short online course covering the basics of nervous-system-friendly breathwork for personal use.
training_programs.long_description:
This three-week introductory course is perfect if you�b4re curious about breathwork but not ready for a full practitioner training. You�b4ll explore basic anatomy, safety considerations, and a handful of short practices you can weave into daily life.

Sessions include live demonstrations, guided practice, and simple homework invitations between weeks.

training_programs.title: 5-Day In-Person Trauma-Informed Retreat Training
training_programs.subtitle: Immersive skills lab for facilitating trauma-aware retreats
training_programs.short_description: An in-person residential training for practitioners who want to design and lead trauma-informed healing retreats.
training_programs.long_description:
Set at one of our retreat centers, this 5-day program blends your own retreat experience with behind-the-scenes training. You�b4ll practice opening and closing circles, managing group nervous systems, and planning for crisis and aftercare.

Daily debriefs unpack real-time facilitation choices, giving you a direct window into the art of holding groups safely.

training_programs.title: 16-Week Online Advanced Trauma Clinic
training_programs.subtitle: Case-based learning for experienced trauma clinicians
training_programs.short_description: A semester-style advanced training using live and recorded case material to deepen complex trauma skills.
training_programs.long_description:
Designed for seasoned therapists and advanced trainees, this 16-week clinic focuses on complex trauma, dissociation, and chronic shame. Each module features a live or recorded case, followed by focused teaching segments and structured discussion.

Participants are invited (but not required) to bring de-identified cases for consultation.

retreat_packages.name: 3-Day Weekend Meditation Immersion
retreat_packages.description: Friday evening to Sunday afternoon meditation immersion in New York City. Includes guided sittings, mindful movement, and group circles, plus simple vegetarian meals.

retreat_packages.name: Saturday Day-Visitor Pass
retreat_packages.description: Day-only access on Saturday, including all meditation sessions, lunch, and afternoon integration circle. No accommodation included.

retreat_packages.name: 3-Day London City Mindfulness Weekend
retreat_packages.description: A calm, small-group mindfulness weekend in London from Friday evening to Sunday afternoon. Includes guided practice blocks and two nights of accommodation.

retreat_packages.name: Core Weekend Program (No Friday Evening)
retreat_packages.description: Join from Saturday morning through Sunday afternoon for the core mindfulness curriculum. One night of accommodation included.

retreat_packages.name: 4-Day Ocean Reset Core Program
retreat_packages.description: A 4-day coastal reset in Lisbon combining daily yoga, mindfulness, and integration circles. Ideal for those needing a short, restorative break.

retreat_packages.name: 4-Day Elemental Reconnection Retreat
retreat_packages.description: Reconnect with the elements through movement, meditation, and time in nature at the Ubud Forest Retreat over four nourishing days.

retreat_packages.name: 5-Day Nervous System Reset Immersion
retreat_packages.description: A 5-day experiential program in Ubud focused on nervous-system education, somatic practice, and supported rest. Price shown is per person based on the most affordable shared accommodation option.

retreat_packages.name: 7-Day Nervous System Reset + Integration
retreat_packages.description: Extend the 5-day immersion with two extra days of supported integration, optional 1:1 sessions, and unstructured rest time.

retreat_packages.name: 5-Day Ocean Mindfulness Retreat
retreat_packages.description: Five days of seaside mindfulness, gentle yoga, and group sharing just outside Lisbon. Price is per person based on the shared quad room option.

retreat_packages.name: Custom Short-Stay (3 Days) Option
retreat_packages.description: Join the Lisbon ocean retreat for any three consecutive days within the scheduled dates. Ideal if you cannot commit to the full 5-day stay.

retreats.title: Weekend Urban Meditation Retreat in New York
retreats.description: A small-group, trauma-sensitive meditation weekend in our New York City Loft Studio. Expect guided sitting practice, mindful movement, and practical tools for integrating calm into everyday life. Designed with anxious beginners and returning practitioners in mind.

retreats.title: London Mindfulness Weekend Retreat
retreats.description: A quiet, city-based mindfulness weekend at the London City Sanctuary. Ideal if you want the depth of retreat practice without leaving the city for long. Includes guided sitting, mindful walking, and reflection circles.
highlights: Gentle yoga suitable for all bodies
Central London location with easy transport links

retreats.title: 4-Day Coastal Nervous System Reset in Lisbon
retreats.description: A four-day coastal reset at our Lisbon Coastal Retreat Center. Mornings focus on yoga and mindfulness, afternoons on rest and optional integration sessions. Perfect if you’re navigating burnout, anxiety, or a life transition.
highlights: Afternoon rest time or coastal walks

retreats.title: 4-Day Elemental Yoga & Nature Retreat in Ubud
retreats.description: Reconnect with earth, water, fire, air, and space in this 4-day yoga and meditation immersion set among the rice fields of Ubud. Includes daily asana, breathwork, and guided time in nature.
highlights: Twice-daily yoga in an open-air shala
Guided nature walks through rice terraces

retreats.title: 5-Day Nervous System Reset Retreat in Ubud
retreats.description: An immersive, trauma-informed 5-day retreat focused on nervous-system education, gentle somatic practices, and supported rest. Ideal for those navigating chronic anxiety, burnout, or post-retreat integration.

retreats.title: 5-Day Ocean Mindfulness Retreat near Lisbon
retreats.description: Five days of seaside mindfulness, gentle yoga, and group inquiry just outside Lisbon. A slower-paced retreat with plenty of spaciousness for solo walks and rest.
highlights: Afternoon coastal walks or quiet time
Evening sharing circles and gentle breathwork

retreats.title: Digital Detox & Rest Weekend in Los Angeles
retreats.description: Step away from screens and into a plant-filled urban sanctuary for a restorative digital detox weekend. Includes guided relaxation, gentle yoga, and time for massage add-ons.
highlights: Rest-friendly schedule with late start times

retreats.title: 7-Day Yoga & Mindfulness Immersion in Ubud
retreats.description: A week-long immersion with twice-daily yoga, mindfulness practice, and ample free time in nature. Suitable for all levels, with trauma-aware teaching.
highlights: Morning dynamic and evening restorative yoga
One free day for local exploration
